channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Violent storms cut through the South and Midwest, spawning tornadoes and killing 6
item.description: LAKE CITY, Ark. (AP) — Tornadoes and violent winds flattened homes and ripped apart buildings from Oklahoma to Indiana in the first round of storms that are expected to bring record-setting rains and life-threatening flash floods across the nation’s midsection in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, GEORGE WALKER IV and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and eliminating diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zac Posen’s GapStudio Collection 01 redefines Gap style with bold tailoring and elegant denim
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As his high-end GapStudio collection makes its debut, fashion designer Zac Posen reflects on his journey to revitalize the Gap brand and his vision for its future. Appointed creative director at Gap Inc.
item.dc:creator: JOHN CARUCCI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Snatched pets and livestock deaths blamed on wolves raise alarms in rural New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Commissioners in a rural New Mexico county say pets are being snatched from front yards and livestock are being maimed and killed by endangered Mexican gray wolves that seem to have no fear of humans, prompting them to consider declaring a state of emergency.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida deputy killed in shootout outside Dollar General store
item.description: A sheriff’s deputy was fatally shot in a shootout with a suspect in Florida on Wednesday, according to authorities in Walton County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Girl, 7, struck, killed by falling boulder at Lake Tahoe ski resort
item.description: A 7-year-old girl was killed by a falling boulder at a popular ski resort in Lake Tahoe over the weekend, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Randall Zúñiga, director of the Judicial Investigation Agency, said they tested the 14-year-old Miller Gardner for carboxyhemoglobin, a compound generated when carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin in the blood.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Girl dies after trip to the dentist
item.description: A 9-year-old girl in California died following a dental surgery, during which she was under general anesthesia, according to the medical examiner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A historic Black church took the Proud Boys to court. Now it controls their trademark
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There is so much history between the walls of Metropolitan African Methodist Episcopal Church, which has hosted funerals for Rosa Parks and Frederick Douglass and opened its pews to American presidents and civil rights icons.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Markets plunge in reaction to Trump 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: Stocks plunged Thursday morning as U.S. trading opened for the first time after President Trump’s announcement of heavy tariffs on nearly every nation exporting products to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Dow drops 1,500 in worldwide sell-off following Trump's tariff shock
item.description: The double-digit tariff hikes rippled through world markets and economists are warning that the risk of recession is climbing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: No bathroom breaks: How Booker accomplished record Senate speech
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) set a new record for the longest Senate speech in history on Monday and Tuesday by holding the upper chamber’s floor for more than 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Beloved Anchorage pet reindeer euthanized after someone tampered with pen, possibly poisoned him
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An iconic pet reindeer beloved by many in Alaska's largest city has been euthanized, just weeks after someone tampered with his cage and possibly poisoned him, his caretaker said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Final report details investigation into deadly 2023 Covenant School shooting
item.description: The report touches on the shooter's planning as well as the details unveiled during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at Trump's DOGE
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — DOGE’s days appear to be numbered. Elon Musk recently suggested that he will be done with his work in the near future. President Donald Trump told reporters this week that “at some point, he’s going to be going back” to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Wild weather expected Wednesday gets a rare 'high-risk' designation
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — U.S. government forecasters are using a relatively rare “high-risk” designation — the highest category they use — to warn that a major tornado outbreak appears likely Wednesday in an area that's home to about 2.5 million people.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rules in favor of FDA in dispute over flavored vapes
item.description: A unanimous Supreme Court overturned a lower court ruling and found the FDA acted lawfully when it blocked two vaping companies from marketing fruity and dessert-flavored liquids for their electronic nicotine products.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: Nintendo announces Switch 2 release date, new features in latest Direct showcase
item.description: After years of waiting, Nintendo fans finally have a release date for the Japanese company's new gaming console.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Mass layoffs rattle Head Start leaders already on edge over funding problems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The problems for Head Start began days after President Donald Trump took office. Trump's administration announced it would freeze federal grants — the primary funding for the early education program that serves more than half a million low-income children.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scientists fear impact of National Weather Service cuts
item.description: As the summer hurricane season looms, so too do cuts to the federal workforce, including the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Forecasters warn of long-lasting tornadoes, deadly floods in parts of US on Wednesday
item.description: Potentially deadly flash flooding, high-magnitude tornadoes and baseball-sized hail could hit parts of the U.S. on Wednesday as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged, forecasters warned.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Chocolate bars recalled over possible small stones
item.description: Dutch chocolate maker Tony's Chocoloney has issued a nationwide recall of two of its chocolate products, which may contain small stones.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Monthly mortgage payments soar to record high
item.description: A typical monthly housing payment hit a record high during the last four weeks ending March 23, according to a study by Redfin.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why Tesla may avoid the blow of Trump's auto tariffs
item.description: Elon Musk’s focus on American-made products at Tesla could shield his company from the brunt of President Donald Trump’s new tariffs on foreign vehicles and automobile parts.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, Fox 5

item.title: April Fool's prank renames French Quarter to American Quarter in New Orleans
item.description: An April Fool’s prank left many New Orleans residents and visitors stunned after a fake website announced that the historic French Quarter had been renamed the “American Quarter” by executive order of Louisiana Gov. Jeff Landry.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Spears, KRON4

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Volcano erupts in southwestern Iceland after a nearby town, spa evacuated
item.description: A volcano in southwestern Iceland that has erupted repeatedly for more than a year again belched lava and smoke into the air on Tuesday, just hours after authorities evacuated the few remaining residents of a nearby fishing village.
item.dc:creator: MARCO Di MARCO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US judge orders Trump administration to restore legal aid to unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in California on Tuesday ordered the Trump administration to temporarily restore legal aid to tens of thousands of migrant children who are in the United States without a parent or guardian.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Astronaut reunites with dogs after 9 months 'stuck' in space
item.description: Touching video shows NASA astronaut Suni Williams reuniting with her dogs after a longer-than-expected stay on board the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: On the heels of a dry winter, firefighters around the US brace for wildfire risks
item.description: PRESCOTT, Ariz. (AP) — From the southwestern U.S. to Minnesota, Iowa and even parts of New Jersey, it seemed that winter never materialized.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's in Florida recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Read is accused of striking her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O’Keefe, with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm outside of a house party.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Miami's Haitian community gathers in prayer as crises escalate in homeland and US
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Packed pews, rollicking singing and emotional devotions have marked Lent worship services at Notre Dame d'Haiti, the Catholic church at the heart of the largest Haitian diaspora in the United States.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Aggressive sea lion attacks 15-year-old girl in Southern California waters
item.description: A 15-year-old in Long Beach, California, is recovering after a terrifying and unexpected ocean encounter with a sea lion that attacked her over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wild weather blamed for deaths of 3 kids in Michigan and a man in an Amish buggy in Indiana
item.description: KALAMAZOO, Mich. (AP) — A tree fell on a van in Michigan, killing three children, during a fierce weekend storm that swept the region and contributed to the death of an 84-year-old man in an Amish buggy in Indiana, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Report: 1 in 5 Catholics in US affected by deportations
item.description: One in five Catholic families in the United States stands to feel the pain from the Trump administration's promised mass deportations, a group of faith leaders said.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5
